items.title: Warp-as-History: Generalizable Camera-Controlled Video Generation from One Training Video
items.summary: Researchers introduce Warp-as-History, a novel method enabling generalizable camera-controlled video generation from a single training video, significantly advancing video synthesis capabilities.

items.title: OpenAI Co-founder Greg Brockman Takes Charge of Product Strategy
items.summary: OpenAI co-founder Greg Brockman is reportedly taking over product strategy, signaling a significant shift in the company's direction and focus.

items.title: ArXiv to Ban Authors for One Year for AI-Generated Submissions
items.summary: The research repository ArXiv announces a new policy to ban authors for a year if they are found to have used AI to generate their entire research papers, aiming to maintain academic integrity.

items.title: OpenAI and Malta Partner to Bring ChatGPT Plus to All Citizens
items.summary: OpenAI has partnered with the government of Malta to provide free access to ChatGPT Plus for all Maltese citizens, marking a significant national-level AI adoption initiative.

items.title: RouteProfile: Elucidating the Design Space of LLM Profiles for Routing
items.summary: This research explores RouteProfile, a framework that clarifies the design space for LLM profiles used in routing, optimizing how language models handle diverse tasks.

items.title: PREPING: Building Agent Memory without Tasks
items.summary: A new paper introduces PREPING, a method for building robust agent memory architectures without requiring explicit task-specific training, enhancing agent adaptability.

items.title: EvolveMem: Self-Evolving Memory Architecture via AutoResearch for LLM Agents
items.summary: EvolveMem proposes a self-evolving memory architecture for LLM agents that uses 'AutoResearch' to continuously improve its memory capabilities, leading to more adaptive AI agents.

items.title: Realiz3D: 3D Generation Made Photorealistic via Domain-Aware Learning
items.summary: Realiz3D presents a new approach to 3D generation that achieves photorealistic results by incorporating domain-aware learning, significantly improving visual fidelity.

items.title: I Ran Hermes Agent on the Same Task for 7 Days. The Skill File on Day 7 Looked Nothing Like Day 1.
items.summary: A developer shares insights from running an AI agent for a week, observing how its 'skill file' dynamically evolved and changed significantly over time, highlighting adaptive learning in agents.

items.title: Long Context Pre-Training with Lighthouse Attention
items.summary: This paper introduces Lighthouse Attention, a novel mechanism designed to enable more efficient and effective long-context pre-training for large language models.

items.title: The Haves and Have Nots of the AI Gold Rush
items.summary: TechCrunch explores the growing disparity between major tech players and smaller entities in the ongoing AI gold rush, examining who benefits most from the current boom.

items.title: Google Partners with XPRIZE and Range Media Partners for $3.5M Future Vision Film Competition
items.summary: Google announces a collaboration with XPRIZE and Range Media Partners to launch the $3.5 million Future Vision film competition, encouraging creative exploration of AI's impact on society.

items.title: My AI Agent Kept Blind-Reading My Files, So I Built a Local CLI to Give It 'Eyes'
items.summary: A developer created a local command-line interface to provide AI agents with controlled access and 'vision' into local files, addressing privacy and efficiency issues with current agent designs.

items.title: Orchard: An Open-Source Agentic Modeling Framework
items.summary: Orchard is introduced as a new open-source framework designed for agentic modeling, providing tools and structures for developing and deploying AI agents.

items.title: I Built an Agent That Actually Reviews Your Pull Requests
items.summary: A developer showcases an AI agent designed to autonomously review pull requests, aiming to streamline code review processes and improve development workflows.
